title: GST Registration
description: GST registration online by CA firm. Regular, non-resident (foreigner), temporary, composition scheme. Expert filing with post-registration compliance.
bodyText:
What is GST registration? Goods and Services Tax registration is mandatory for businesses exceeding threshold turnover (₹40 lakhs for goods, ₹20 lakhs for services, ₹10 lakhs for special category states) under Section 22 of the CGST Act, 2017. Registration provides a 15-digit GSTIN required for collecting tax, claiming Input Tax Credit, and filing returns. Virtual Auditor handles the complete GST registration process with ongoing compliance support — return filing, ITC optimisation, and appeal representation if needed. Quick Answer: GST Registration — GST registration online by CA firm. Regular, non-resident (foreigner), temporary, composition scheme. Expert filing with post-registration compliance. 3-7 days.
GST Registration is a service offered by Virtual Auditor, an AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333) led by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV), specialising in GST compliance and appellate representation, from offices in Chennai, Bangalore, and Mumbai since 2012.
Source: CGST Act 2017, CGST Rules 2017, CBIC Circulars Official References: GST Portal ↗ · CBIC Acts & Rules ↗
HEADING3: Types of GST Registration
Regular Registration (Section 22) Non-Resident (Section 24(v)) Temporary Registration Composition Scheme (Section 10) Input Service Distributor (ISD) Casual Taxable Person E-Commerce Operator (Section 24(ix)) TDS/TCS Registration SEZ Developer/Unit Voluntary Registration (Section 25(3))
HEADING3: When Is GST Registration Mandatory?
Regulatory basis: Sections 22-24, CGST Act 2017. Registration within 30 days of becoming liable. Voluntary registration available under Section 25(3) regardless of turnover.
Mandatory registration (regardless of turnover): (a) E-commerce sellers (Section 24(ix)), (b) persons making inter-state taxable supply, (c) casual taxable persons, (d) non-resident taxable persons, (e) persons liable to pay tax under reverse charge, (f) agents of registered suppliers, (g) ISD, and (h) persons deducting TDS under GST.

Threshold-based registration: Aggregate turnover exceeding ₹40 lakhs (goods) or ₹20 lakhs (services) in a financial year. ₹10 lakhs for special category states (Manipur, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura).

HEADING3: Documents Required

PAN of business entity, Aadhaar of proprietor/partners/directors, photograph, address proof of business premises (rent agreement + NOC or ownership document), bank statement or cancelled cheque, electricity bill of premises, and constitution documents (partnership deed, MOA/AOA for companies, LLP agreement).

HEADING2: Why Register Through a CA Firm vs. Self-Filing

You can file GST registration yourself on the GST portal. So why pay a CA firm?

1. Application rejection prevention: 15-20% of self-filed applications face queries or rejection due to incorrect HSN/SAC classification, incomplete address proof, or document format issues. Our CA team knows the exact requirements and formats that each state GST office expects — avoiding the rejection-refile-rejection cycle.

2. Post-registration compliance: Registration is day one of an ongoing compliance obligation. Monthly/quarterly returns (GSTR-1, GSTR-3B), annual return (GSTR-9), ITC reconciliation (GSTR-2A/2B matching), e-invoicing (if applicable), and e-way bills. Miss a deadline: ₹50-200/day penalty. We set up the compliance calendar from day one.

3. Future-proofing: Many businesses register under the wrong category or wrong state, creating complications when they expand. We advise on: regular vs. composition scheme, single vs. multi-state registration, ISD requirements, and whether voluntary registration makes sense for ITC recovery.

4. GST appeal readiness: If the department issues a demand, the same team that handled your registration — understanding your business model, transaction patterns, and compliance history — handles the appeal. No re-explaining your business to a new professional.

HEADING2: GST Registration for E-Commerce Sellers

E-commerce sellers face unique GST requirements under Section 24(ix):

Mandatory registration: Every person supplying through an e-commerce operator must register, regardless of turnover. The threshold exemption does not apply.

TCS compliance: E-commerce operators (Amazon, Flipkart, Meesho) deduct TCS at 1% of net taxable value. Sellers must claim this as credit in their returns.

Multi-state complications: If you sell across states through e-commerce, you may need registration in each state where you have a physical presence (warehouse, fulfilment centre). Even FBA (Fulfilled by Amazon) creates presence in states where Amazon warehouses hold your inventory.

We advise on: single vs. multi-state registration strategy, TCS reconciliation, input credit optimisation for e-commerce sellers, and compliance structuring to minimise multi-state burden.

HEADING2: Composition Scheme — Simplified Compliance

The Composition Scheme under Section 10 offers simplified compliance for small taxpayers:

Eligibility: Turnover up to ₹1.5 crore (goods manufacturers/traders) or ₹50 lakhs (service providers). Not available for: inter-state suppliers, e-commerce sellers, ice cream/tobacco/pan masala manufacturers, and casual/non-resident taxable persons.

Tax rates: Manufacturers: 1% of turnover. Traders: 1%. Restaurants: 5%. Service providers: 6%. These are flat rates — no input tax credit available.

Compliance: Quarterly return (GSTR-4) instead of monthly. Annual return (GSTR-4 Annual). No e-invoicing requirement. No e-way bill generation (but must carry e-way bill if received by a regular dealer).

Trade-off: Simpler compliance but no ITC means higher effective tax cost. Suitable for businesses with low input costs (services, retail) where ITC benefit is minimal. Not suitable for businesses with high input costs (manufacturing with raw material purchases) where ITC loss outweighs compliance savings.

We advise on: composition vs. regular registration economics, threshold monitoring (mandatory conversion to regular if turnover crosses ₹1.5 crore), and transition filing requirements.

HEADING2: GST Registration for Non-Residents and Foreigners

Non-resident taxable persons and foreigners can register under GST (Section 24(v)) with specific requirements:

Application: Filed at least 5 days before commencing business in India. Registration valid for the period specified (minimum 1 day, maximum 90 days, extendable).

Advance tax deposit: Equal to the estimated tax liability for the registration period. Deposited at the time of application.

Authorised signatory: Must appoint an authorised person in India as a signatory for GST filings.

GSTIN format: Same 15-digit structure. First 2 digits: state code of the state where the non-resident intends to supply.

Practical application: Foreign companies exhibiting at Indian trade shows, executing projects in India, or providing services from a temporary establishment. Also applicable to foreign e-commerce operators serving Indian customers.

Virtual Auditor handles GST registration for foreign companies and non-resident individuals, including authorised representative arrangement and advance tax deposit computation.

HEADING2: State-Wise GST Registration Nuances

While GST is a national tax, the registration process has state-specific nuances that cause confusion and rejection:

Tamil Nadu: Strict address proof requirements — must be a commercial premises or residential with specific commercial usage permission from the municipality. Aadhaar authentication may face OTP delays in certain districts.

Karnataka: Bangalore-specific: many co-working space addresses face additional scrutiny. The officer may schedule a physical verification visit. We advise on address documentation that satisfies Karnataka GST officers.

Maharashtra: Mumbai registrations face high volume — processing times may be longer (7-10 days vs. 3-5 in other states). Additional documentation for Shops and Establishments Act number may be requested.

Multi-state registration: Businesses operating in multiple states need separate GSTIN per state. Each registration requires a state-specific address proof and authorised signatory. We handle multi-state registrations as a package, ensuring consistent classification (HSN/SAC codes) across all states to avoid reconciliation issues in GST returns.

Virtual Auditor has office presence in Tamil Nadu (Chennai), Karnataka (Bangalore), and Maharashtra (Mumbai), giving us firsthand knowledge of state-specific GST registration requirements and officer expectations in these three major commercial states.

HEADING3: GST Registration — Regular vs Composition

Parameter | Regular | Composition Scheme |
Threshold | ₹40L goods / ₹20L services | ₹1.5 Cr goods / ₹50L services |
ITC available | Yes | No |
Returns | GSTR-1 + GSTR-3B (monthly/quarterly) | CMP-08 (quarterly) |
Tax rate | Slab rates (5-28%) | Flat 1-6% |
Interstate supply | Allowed | Not allowed |
HEADING2: People Also Ask

HEADING3: What is the GST registration threshold in India?

Mandatory for turnover above ₹40 lakhs (goods) or ₹20 lakhs (services). ₹10 lakhs for special category states. Compulsory regardless of turnover for e-commerce sellers and interstate suppliers.

HEADING3: What is the penalty for late GST return filing?

Late fee of ₹50/day (₹20 for nil returns) under Section 47, capped at ₹5,000 per return period. Interest at 18% p.a. on tax liability under Section 50.

HEADING3: ⚡ How Virtual Auditor Delivers This Differently

Our AI-assisted registration analyser pre-validates your application against common rejection triggers — incorrect HSN codes, address proof mismatches, principal place of business discrepancies, and Aadhaar authentication failures — before submission to the GST portal — delivering a data-driven contest-or-accept recommendation within 24 hours.

HEADING3: Latest Regulatory Updates (FY 2025-26)
This page has been updated to reflect changes introduced in Budget 2025, recent notifications from CBDT, CBIC, MCA, SEBI, and RBI, and evolving compliance requirements for FY 2025-26. Virtual Auditor continuously monitors regulatory developments to ensure all advice and filings are current and compliant with the latest provisions.
HEADING2: Recent Engagement — How We Helped
Context: a manufacturing company with operations across 4 states facing a GST demand order for alleged irregular ITC claims.
Challenge: The company received a demand notice for disallowance of Input Tax Credit worth Rs 45 lakhs on inter-state purchases, citing mismatch between GSTR-2A and GSTR-3B returns, along with a 100% penalty under Section 74 for alleged suppression.
Our approach: We conducted a line-by-line reconciliation of purchase invoices against GSTR-2A data, identified that the mismatch was due to delayed filing by 3 suppliers, and prepared a comprehensive reply with supplier-wise reconciliation statements, payment proofs, and case law references supporting ITC eligibility under Section 16(2).
Outcome: The Appellate Authority reduced the demand by 85%, accepting our reconciliation evidence. The penalty was dropped entirely as no suppression was established. Total savings for the client: Rs 38 lakhs in tax demand plus Rs 45 lakhs in penalty.
This engagement illustrates Virtual Auditor's approach to gst registration — combining regulatory expertise with practical execution to deliver results within the client's timeline.
HEADING2: What You Receive
Upon completion of the gst registration engagement, you will receive: Completed GST form/filing acknowledgment, computation worksheet with detailed working, reconciliation statement where applicable, compliance calendar for upcoming due dates, and a summary advisory note highlighting any issues or opportunities identified during the engagement.
All deliverables are reviewed by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV) before release to ensure accuracy and regulatory compliance.

faqs.q: How long does GST registration take?
faqs.a: 3-7 working days from application submission, assuming documents are in order and Aadhaar authentication is successful. Rejection or query from the officer may extend the timeline.

faqs.q: Is GST registration mandatory for e-commerce sellers?
faqs.a: Yes. Under Section 24(ix), every person supplying goods/services through an e-commerce operator must register regardless of turnover threshold.

faqs.q: Can a foreigner get GST registration in India?
faqs.a: Yes. Non-resident taxable persons register under Section 24(v). Registration valid for the period specified (minimum 1 day). Advance tax deposit equal to estimated liability required.
Section 10 allows small taxpayers (turnover up to ₹1.5 crore for goods, ₹50 lakhs for services) to pay tax at a flat rate (1-6%) without ITC. Simpler compliance — quarterly returns.

faqs.q: Why register through a CA firm instead of doing it myself?
faqs.a: Registration is day one. What follows is monthly return filing, ITC reconciliation, annual return, and potential notices/appeals. A CA firm provides the complete lifecycle — registration, compliance, and defence. We are also positioned to handle GST appeals if any dispute arises.

faqs.q: What is the Input Service Distributor (ISD) registration?
faqs.a: ISD registration under Section 24(viii) is required when a head office or corporate office receives common invoices for services (rent, professional fees, software licences) consumed by multiple branches in different states. The ISD distributes the input tax credit to respective branch GSTINs proportionally. Mandatory if your business has centralised procurement with multi-state operations.

faqs.q: Can GST registration be cancelled voluntarily?
faqs.a: Yes. Apply for cancellation via Form GST REG-16 on the GST portal if: business is discontinued, transferred, or turnover falls below threshold. Final return (GSTR-10) must be filed within 3 months of cancellation. We handle the cancellation process including ITC reversal computation and final return filing.
